message:
Add RFC 6587 octet-counting support to parse_syslog (#5674)

## Summary

The `parse_syslog` function now supports RFC 6587 octet-counted framing,
where syslog messages are prefixed with their byte length (for example,
`65 <syslog-message>`). This framing is commonly used in TCP-based
syslog transport to handle message boundaries.

## Changes

- Add `octet_counting` parameter to `parse_syslog` with three modes:
- **Not specified (default)**: Auto-detect and strip length prefix if
present and valid
- **`octet_counting=true`**: Require a length prefix, emit warning if
missing
  - **`octet_counting=false`**: Never strip a length prefix
- Introduce `octet_length_parser`, a shared parser combinator used by
both `parse_syslog` (single-value) and `read_syslog` (streaming) for
consistent behavior
- Add integration tests covering all three modes

## Design decisions

- Auto-detection validates that the stated length matches the remaining
content exactly, preventing false positives where input coincidentally
starts with `<digits><space>`
- Uses `uint32_t` for the length field per RFC 6587's grammar
(`NONZERO-DIGIT *DIGIT`)

## Test plan

- [x] Manual testing of all three `octet_counting` modes
- [x] Integration tests pass (4 new test cases)
- [x] Build succeeds

## Related PRs

- Docs: https://github.com/tenzir/docs/pull/167

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+---
+title: RFC 6587 octet-counting support for syslog parsing
+type: feature
+authors:
+  - mavam
+  - claude
+created: 2026-01-21T07:41:37.394369Z
+---
+
+The `parse_syslog` function now supports RFC 6587 octet-counted framing, where syslog messages are prefixed with their byte length (for example, `65 <syslog-message>`). This framing is commonly used in TCP-based syslog transport to handle message boundaries.
+
+The new `octet_counting` parameter for `parse_syslog` offers three modes:
+
+- **Not specified (default)**: Auto-detect. The parser strips a length prefix if present and valid, otherwise parses the input as-is. This prevents false positives where input coincidentally starts with digits and a space.
+- **`octet_counting=true`**: Require a length prefix. Emits a warning and returns null if the input lacks a valid prefix.
+- **`octet_counting=false`**: Never strip a length prefix. Parse the input as-is.

@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+// Test octet counting in parse_syslog
+// - Auto-detect with octet-counted input (65 bytes after the space)
+// - Auto-detect with regular syslog (no prefix)
+from { x: "65 <165>1 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z host app 1234 ID01 - Test message" },
+     { x: "<165>1 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z host app 1234 ID01 - Test message" }
+x = x.parse_syslog()

@@ -0,0 +1,28 @@
+{
+  x: {
+    facility: 20,
+    severity: 5,
+    version: 1,
+    timestamp: 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z,
+    hostname: "host",
+    app_name: "app",
+    process_id: "1234",
+    message_id: "ID01",
+    structured_data: {},
+    message: "Test message",
+  },
+}
+{
+  x: {
+    facility: 20,
+    severity: 5,
+    version: 1,
+    timestamp: 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z,
+    hostname: "host",
+    app_name: "app",
+    process_id: "1234",
+    message_id: "ID01",
+    structured_data: {},
+    message: "Test message",
+  },
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+// Test explicit octet_counting=false (never strips prefix)
+from { x: "<165>1 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z host app 1234 ID01 - Test message" }
+x = x.parse_syslog(octet_counting=false)

@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+{
+  x: {
+    facility: 20,
+    severity: 5,
+    version: 1,
+    timestamp: 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z,
+    hostname: "host",
+    app_name: "app",
+    process_id: "1234",
+    message_id: "ID01",
+    structured_data: {},
+    message: "Test message",
+  },
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+// Test explicit octet_counting=true (expects prefix, warns if not found)
+from { x: "65 <165>1 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z host app 1234 ID01 - Test message" }
+x = x.parse_syslog(octet_counting=true)

@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+{
+  x: {
+    facility: 20,
+    severity: 5,
+    version: 1,
+    timestamp: 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z,
+    hostname: "host",
+    app_name: "app",
+    process_id: "1234",
+    message_id: "ID01",
+    structured_data: {},
+    message: "Test message",
+  },
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,4 @@
+// octet_counting=false should NOT strip numeric prefix
+// Input starting with digits should be parsed as-is (will fail syslog parse)
+from { x: "65 <165>1 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z host app 1234 ID01 - Test message" }
+x = x.parse_syslog(octet_counting=false)

@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+{
+  x: null,
+}
+warning: `input` is not valid syslog
+ --> tests/functions/parse_syslog/octet_counting_false_with_prefix.tql:4:5
+  |
+4 | x = x.parse_syslog(octet_counting=false)
+  |     ~ 
+  |

@@ -0,0 +1,4 @@
+// Auto-detect with mismatched length prefix (10 != actual 65)
+// In auto mode, full message parses successfully; warns that prefix was ignored
+from { x: "10 <165>1 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z host app 1234 ID01 - Test message" }
+x = x.parse_syslog()

@@ -0,0 +1,21 @@
+{
+  x: {
+    facility: 20,
+    severity: 5,
+    version: 1,
+    timestamp: 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z,
+    hostname: "host",
+    app_name: "app",
+    process_id: "1234",
+    message_id: "ID01",
+    structured_data: {},
+    message: "Test message",
+  },
+}
+warning: octet count prefix ignored
+ --> tests/functions/parse_syslog/octet_counting_length_mismatch.tql:4:5
+  |
+4 | x = x.parse_syslog()
+  |     ~ 
+  |
+  = note: message parsed without framing

@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+// Test octet_counting=true with input missing the octet prefix (should warn)
+from { x: "<165>1 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z host app 1234 ID01 - Test message" }
+x = x.parse_syslog(octet_counting=true)

@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+{
+  x: null,
+}
+warning: expected valid octet-counted input
+ --> tests/functions/parse_syslog/octet_counting_missing.tql:3:5
+  |
+3 | x = x.parse_syslog(octet_counting=true)
+  |     ~ 
+  |

@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+// Zero-length message prefix (RFC 6587 requires NONZERO-DIGIT)
+from { x: "0 " }
+x = x.parse_syslog(octet_counting=true)

@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+{
+  x: null,
+}
+warning: `input` is not valid syslog
+ --> tests/functions/parse_syslog/octet_counting_zero_length.tql:3:5
+  |
+3 | x = x.parse_syslog(octet_counting=true)
+  |     ~ 
+  |
